records.ety: [F. notifier, L. notificare; notus known (p. p. of noscere to known) + -ficare (in comp.) to make. See Know, and -fy.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Notified (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Notifying (?).]

defs: To make known; to declare; to publish; as, to notify a fact to a person.
quotes: No law can bind till it be notified or promulged.

defs: To give notice to; to inform by notice; to apprise; as, the constable has notified the citizens to meet at the city hall; the bell notifies us of the time of meeting.
quotes: The President of the United States has notified the House of Representatives that he has approved and signed the act.
notes: ☞ This application of notify has been condemned; but it is in constant good use in the United States, and in perfect accordance with the use of certify.
